plays.title: Völund's Lay
plays.description: Völund the captive smith is crippled by King Níduðr, but exacts a terrible revenge and escapes on artificial wings.

plays.title: The First Lay of Helgi Hunding's Slayer
plays.description: Helgi defeats King Hunding in battle and pledges his eternal love to the valkyrie Sigrún.

plays.title: The Lay of Helgi Hjörvard's Slayer
plays.description: Helgi receives his name and a magic sword from the valkyrie Sváva, but is doomed to fall in a tragic duel.

plays.title: Sinfiotli's Death
plays.description: Sinfiotli is poisoned by his stepmother Queen Borghild, who offers him a deadly horn of ale.

plays.title: Sigurd's Lay
plays.description: Sigurd slays the dragon Fáfnir on the Gnita-heath and gains the cursed hoard of the Nibelungs.

plays.title: Grimild's Revenge
plays.description: Grimild invites her brothers to her hall and plots a bloody slaughter to avenge the murder of her husband Sivard.

plays.title: King Dieterich and the Dragon
plays.description: King Dieterich encounters a giant dragon in the wild forest and fights it with the help of a loyal lion.

plays.title: Vidrich and Langbein
plays.description: Vidrich confronts the giant Langbein at a stone bridge and duels him in a show of defiance.

plays.title: Sivard's Death
plays.description: Sivard rides his horse over the glass mountain, but falls victim to Queen Brynhild's murderous plot.

plays.title: The Royal Shepherdess
plays.description: A disguised princess works as a shepherdess until a golden ring reveals her true royal identity to the king.

plays.title: Das Hildebrandslied
plays.description: The tragic duel between Hildebrand and his son Hadubrand.

plays.title: Der Hürnen Seyfrid
plays.description: Seyfrid's dragon fight and acquisition of the horny skin.

plays.title: Walther und Hildegund
plays.description: The flight of Walther and Hildegund and their defense in the Wasgenwald.

plays.title: Biterolf und Dietleib
plays.description: The quest of Dietleib to find his father Biterolf and their reunion at Etzel's court.

plays.title: Ortnit
plays.description: King Ortnit's tragic death due to the dragon eggs sent by Machorel.

plays.title: Introduction
plays.description: Historical and linguistic context of Germanic myth, modeling the migration and evolution of folk tradition.

plays.title: God
plays.description: The transition from pagan polytheism to monotheism, modeling the shift in divine concepts.

plays.title: Worship
plays.description: Sacrificial rites, worship practices, and prayer in Germanic antiquity.

plays.title: Temples
plays.description: Sacred architecture, halls, and temples of Germanic worship.

plays.title: Sacred Places
plays.description: Natural loci of worship: sacred groves, hills, and wells.

plays.title: Priests
plays.description: Mediators and holy men, modeling the priestly and prophetic roles in Germanic antiquity.

plays.title: Gods
plays.description: The pagan pantheon in general, modeling the divine community and its attributes.

plays.title: Wuotan
plays.description: Wuotan, Wodan, Odin: the supreme deity and leader of the wild hunt.

plays.title: Donar
plays.description: Donar, Thonar, Thor: the thunder-lord and protector of Midgard.

plays.title: Tiu
plays.description: Tiu, Zio, Tyr: the sword-god and lord of the assembly.

plays.title: Fro
plays.description: Fro, Phol, Freyr: the lord of fertility, peace, and abundance.

plays.title: Other Gods
plays.description: Ostara, Baldr, and lesser gods, modeling spring, light, and death.

plays.title: Goddesses
plays.description: Frigg, Freyja, Bertha, and Holda: the protective and domestic goddesses.

plays.title: Condition of Gods
plays.description: Characteristics, lifespan, and realms of the gods, focusing on fate.

plays.title: Heroes
plays.description: Demigods, giants, and ancestral heroes, modeling mythic quests.

plays.title: Wise Women
plays.description: Witches, sibyls, and wood-wives, modeling prophetic and magical women.

plays.title: Wights and Elves
plays.description: Dwarfs, wights, and house-spirits, modeling underground craftsmanship and magic.

plays.title: Giants
plays.description: Riesen, Hünen, and primeval forces in conflict with the gods.

plays.title: Creation
plays.description: Cosmogony, Yggdrasil, and the shaping of the world.

plays.title: Elements
plays.description: Fire, Water, Air, and Earth as active cosmological forces.

plays.title: Trees and Plants
plays.description: Sacred flora, world-pillars, and herbal magic in folk belief.

plays.title: Sky and Stars
plays.description: Celestial bodies, constellations, and atmospheric phenomena.

plays.title: Day and Night
plays.description: Sacred time, divisions of the day, and the twilight.

plays.title: Year and Seasons
plays.description: The seasonal cycle, solstices, and agricultural festivals.

plays.title: Souls
plays.description: The soul of man, breath, shadow, and journey to the afterlife.

plays.title: Death
plays.description: Mortality, funeral pyres, urn burials, and the guidance of souls.

plays.title: Fate and Destiny
plays.description: Norns, weird, and the loom of destiny.

plays.title: Personifications
plays.description: Abstract concepts and natural forces made flesh.

plays.title: Poetry
plays.description: Sacred song, skaldenmet, and divine language.

plays.title: Spectres
plays.description: Ghosts, wraiths, and the return of the dead.

plays.title: Translation
plays.description: Shape-shifting and animal transformation in folk legend.

plays.title: Devil
plays.description: The pagan legacy and attributes in the christianized devil.

plays.title: Magic
plays.description: Zauberei, divination, and sorcery in ancient belief.

plays.title: Superstition
plays.description: Folk customs, residual paganism, and domestic superstitions.

plays.title: Spells and Charms
plays.description: Incantations, verbal formulas, and bless-sayings.

plays.title: Admonitions and Vows
plays.description: Oaths, taboos, sworn pacts, and solemn vows in old law.

plays.title: Curses and Blessings
plays.description: Verbal magic, protective blessings, and destructive curses.

plays.title: Appendix
plays.description: Residual fragments, superstitions, and final chronicle entries.

plays.title: The Three Miners in the Kuttenberg
plays.description: Three pious miners are trapped in a mine for seven years until their final wishes are granted.

plays.title: The Mountain Spirit
plays.description: The mountain spirit Meister Hämmerling appears in various forms in the silver mines to punish or reward miners.

plays.title: The Mountain Monk in the Harz
plays.description: Two miners are saved from darkness and helped by a giant monk, until their drunken boast makes the magic oil vanish.

plays.title: Mother Holle's Pond
plays.description: The pond on Hoher Meißner acts as the gateway to Mother Holle's underworld castle where she rewards the industrious.

plays.title: Mother Holle Wanders
plays.description: During the twelve nights of Christmas, Mother Holle wanders to reward industrious spinners and punish the lazy.

plays.title: Mother Holle's Bath
plays.description: At noon, Mother Holle bathes in her sacred pool on Hoher Meißner, demanding respect from all who pass by.

plays.title: Frau Holla and Faithful Eckart
plays.description: The legend of Frau Holla passing through Schwarza accompanied by the faithful Eckart who warns travelers.

plays.title: Frau Holla and the Peasant
plays.description: Frau Holla rewards a helpful peasant who repairs her carriage wheel with wood shavings that turn into pure gold.

plays.title: The Springing Root
plays.description: A shepherd uses a magical spring-root to open the locked doors inside the Köterberg and gather treasures.

plays.title: The Maidens of Boyneburg
plays.description: Three noble maidens on the Boyneburg await a predicted death by lightning, showing submission to God's will.

plays.title: The Pielberg
plays.description: A beautiful banished virgin appears at noon on the Pielberg, warning people to stay away.

plays.title: The Castle Maiden
plays.description: A maiden carrying a large keyring appears at noon on the Schlossberg, bathing in the fountain before returning.

plays.title: The Snake Maiden
plays.description: Leonhard enters a subterranean vault where a half-snake maiden promises treasures, but he flees in terror.

plays.title: The Heavy Child
plays.description: Two noblemen find a child in a bush that cannot be lifted, which prophesies a fruitful but deadly year.

plays.title: The Old Wine Cellar near Salurn
plays.description: Christoph finds a wine cellar guarded by three old men, but after revealing the secret, he is killed.

plays.title: Giants' Game
plays.description: Giants playing on the Brunsberg throw a ball into the valley, forming the Knäuelwiese before their demise.

plays.title: The Giants' Toy
plays.description: A giant's daughter takes a ploughing peasant as a toy, but her father teaches her to respect the humans who feed them.

plays.title: Giant Einheer
plays.description: The giant warrior Einheer wades through rivers, pulls his horse by the tail, and fights like an entire army.

plays.title: Giants' Pillars
plays.description: Nine stone pillars in the forest near Miltenberg bear the handprints of giants who tried to build a bridge.

plays.title: The Köterberg
plays.description: A shepherd finds the entrance to gold inside the Köterberg but is chased away by a bloody man.

plays.title: Geroldseck
plays.description: Ancient German heroes rest inside Castle Geroldseck, waiting to emerge and save their people in times of need.

plays.title: Kaiser Karl at Nuremberg
plays.description: Kaiser Karl sits in the deep well of Nuremberg Castle, his beard grown through a stone table.

plays.title: Barbarossa in the Kyffhaeuser
plays.description: Emperor Frederick Barbarossa sleeps inside the Kyffhäuser, awaiting the day the ravens stop flying to return.

plays.title: The Pear Tree on Walserfeld
plays.description: A dry pear tree on the Walserfeld predicts a terrible final battle when it greens and bears fruit.

plays.title: The Enchanted King at Schildheiss
plays.description: Workers find an enchanted king and a maiden in Castle Schildheiß who turns into a fire-breathing snake.

plays.title: Kaiser Karl's Departure
plays.description: Before a war, the Odenberg opens and Kaiser Karl emerges with his army, sounding his horn.

plays.title: The Untersberg
plays.description: The Untersberg near Salzburg contains palaces, churches, and treasures guarded by small midnight-walking dwarfs.

plays.title: Kaiser Karl in the Untersberg
plays.description: Kaiser Karl sits in the Untersberg with a golden crown, his beard growing around the table until the end of the world.

plays.title: Scherfenberger and the Dwarf
plays.description: Knight Wilhelm von Scherfenberg receives a protective ring from a dwarf king, but dies on the battlefield.

plays.title: The Silent People at Plesse
plays.description: Helpful dwarfs called the silent people live in Castle Plesse, punishing human offenses through their cattle.

plays.title: The Little People's Wedding
plays.description: Dwarfs slip into Eilenburg Castle at night to celebrate a wedding, inviting the old Count to join.

plays.title: Dwarfs Turned to Stone
plays.description: In a valley near Elnbogen, helpful dwarfs are turned to stone by their angry ruler when they go to a wedding.

plays.title: Dwarf Mountains
plays.description: Near Aachen, Jena, and Hohenstein, dwarfs borrow kitchen utensils from humans for their weddings and return them.

plays.title: Dwarfs Borrow Bread
plays.description: A dwarf woman borrows fresh bread from a farmer, returning a warm loaf, but warns that human sins drive them away.

plays.title: The Count of Hoia
plays.description: A dwarf asks the Count of Hoia to host a nightly feast in his castle, rewarding him with a sword and a ring.

plays.title: Dwarfs Driven Out
plays.description: The noise of hammers and stamp mills in the Ore Mountains drives the dwarfs away, though they promise to return.

plays.title: The Wichts
plays.description: Tiny dwarfs dressed as miners haunt the pits, teasing workers and knocking three times as an omen of death.

plays.title: Conjuring the Dwarfs
plays.description: Paul Creuz conjures dwarfs using milk, honey, and a black hen, eventually calling their King for a book of wisdom.

plays.title: The Dwarf at the Dance
plays.description: A dwarf from the Wunderberg joins a wedding in Glaß, dances with the maidens, and demands honorable behavior.

plays.title: The Cellar Dwarf
plays.description: A cellar dwarf in Lützen prays alongside a maid; later, a fire destroys the town but spares his house.

plays.title: The Ancestress of Rantzau
plays.description: A dwarf leads the grandmother of Rantzau to help a Zwergin give birth, rewarding her with gold under strict warnings.

plays.title: Hermann of Rosenberg
plays.description: After Hermann von Rosenberg's feast, earth spirits request to hold their own wedding in his hall, promising peace.

plays.title: The Osenberg Dwarfs
plays.description: Osenberg dwarfs pay for beer with silver; a forgotten jug brings luck to the family until it breaks.

plays.title: The Earth Spirit and the Shepherd Boy
plays.description: A shepherd boy frees a dwarf trapped in a stone; the dwarf moves into a neighbor's house, refusing to leave.

plays.title: The Lodging Dwarf
plays.description: A wet dwarf seeks shelter in a storm; only a poor pious couple takes him in, saving them from a devastating landslide.

plays.title: Zeitelmoos
plays.description: A rider meets two children laughing in the Zeitelmoos forest; they reappear ahead of him, laughing mockingly.

plays.title: The Moss Woman
plays.description: A woodcutter Hans Krepel refuses a moss woman's plea to carve crosses in the last tree stump, triggering her wrath.

plays.title: The Wild Hunt and the Moss People
plays.description: The Moss People dress in green moss to hide from the Wild Hunt that storms through the forests of Saalfeld.

plays.title: The Water Sprite
plays.description: A midwife is summoned to help the Water Sprite's wife; she is warned to accept only modest pay or face death.

plays.title: The Wild Women of the Untersberg
plays.description: Wild women emerge from the Untersberg to feed shepherd children and assist with the harvest before returning.

plays.title: Dance with the Water Sprite
plays.description: A water sprite with an icy hand dances with Ursula at a festival in Laibach, eventually dragging her into the river.

plays.title: The Water Sprite and the Farmer
plays.description: A friendly water sprite invites his neighboring farmer to visit his home beneath the lake.

plays.title: The Water Sprite at the Butcher's Bench
plays.description: A butcher cuts the finger of a water sprite who always pays for meat with holed coins, causing him to vanish.

plays.title: The Swimmer
plays.description: A boastful baker's apprentice bets he can swim across the Elbe three times, only to be dragged down by a Nix.

plays.title: Brother Nickel
plays.description: Fishermen on Rügen find their boat placed high in a tree by a hidden water spirit named Brother Nickel.

plays.title: The Nix Well
plays.description: Nixes appear at the Nix Well near Kirchhain, and another nix is sighted at the Elisabeth mill in Marburg.

plays.title: The Magdeburg Nixes
plays.description: A nix drags a skilled swimmer into the depths of the Elbe and later thwarts the construction of a water pipe.

plays.title: The Dönges Lake
plays.description: Two nixes flee a village festival after a youth steals their gloves, causing the lake to turn blood-red.

plays.title: Mummelsee
plays.description: Throwing stones into the bottomless Mummelsee triggers severe storms until water sprites return the stones.

plays.title: The Elbe Maiden and the Saale Woman
plays.description: Nixes disguised as clean town girls shop at meat markets, recognizable only by a wet corner of their aprons.

plays.title: Water Right
plays.description: A river claims its annual human sacrifice at Leipzig, while nixes dance on the water before a drowning.

plays.title: The Drowned Child
plays.description: A grieving mother collects her drowned child's bones from the lake; when placed on the altar, the child revives.

plays.title: Slit-Ears
plays.description: A malicious water spirit named Slit-Ears lurks in the river Streu to drag crossing travelers to their deaths.

plays.title: The Water Nix and the Miller's Apprentice
plays.description: An apprentice shoots at a nix combing her hair on the river; three days later, he drowns while bathing.

plays.title: Before the Nixes Oregano and Horehound Help
plays.description: A midwife helping a nix-wife give birth under the Saale river uses protective herbs to escape the nix's power.

plays.title: The Nix's Legs
plays.description: A barber shines a light on a nix's legs at midnight, exposing the water spirit and forcing it to vanish.

plays.title: The Maid and the Nix
plays.description: A maid serves a nix under the river for three years, leaving because the food is always completely unsalted.

plays.title: The Lady of Alvensleben
plays.description: A noblewoman assists a nix-wife giving birth inside a mountain, receiving a golden ring that seals her lineage's fate.

plays.title: The Lady of Hahn and the Nix
plays.description: A noblewoman helping a nix-wife under the river refuses ashes, earning three gold pieces that sustain her house.

plays.title: The Lady of Bonikau
plays.description: A lady in childbed allows earthmen to celebrate a wedding in her room, hosting an invisible festive assembly.

plays.title: The Measuring Laddle, the Ring and the Cup
plays.description: A dying count divides his lands and leaves three magical fairy gifts to his daughters to secure their fortunes.

plays.title: The Kobold
plays.description: A helpful kobold assists a household with various chores, bringing fortune as long as he is treated well.

plays.title: The Peasant and His Kobold
plays.description: A peasant tries to burn down his barn to escape a kobold, only to find the spirit riding along on his cart.

plays.title: The Kobold in the Mill
plays.description: Two students shelter in a mill where one eats the food set out for the house spirit, earning a midnight thrashing.

plays.title: Little Hat
plays.description: A house spirit wearing a small felt hat serves the bishop of Hildesheim, but takes bloody revenge when mocked.

plays.title: Hinzelmann
plays.description: The house spirit Hinzelmann haunts Castle Hudemühlen, keeping house, speaking to guests, and giving warnings.

plays.title: Knocker
plays.description: A helpful household spirit in Flügelau Castle flees in a trail of fire when forced to reveal his true form.

plays.title: Boot
plays.description: A wounded castle spirit in Calenberg wears a large boot on one leg, fearing it might be torn off.

plays.title: Ekerken
plays.description: A roadway spirit near Elten plagues travelers, remaining invisible except for a single human hand.

plays.title: Night Spirit of Kendenich
plays.description: A ghostly nun hiding in the swamp near Kendenich leaps onto the backs of travelers and rides them all night.

plays.title: The Nightmare
plays.description: The nightmare spirit slips through tiny cracks to press on sleepers' chests, fleeing only if one moves a thumb.

plays.title: The Changeling
plays.description: A mother discovers her crying, insatiable child is a changeling when a voice from a bridge calls out to it.

plays.title: The Changelings in the Water
plays.description: A peasant carrying an insatiable changeling in a basket throws it in the river when the creature speaks.

plays.title: The Mandrake
plays.description: A mandrake root, grown under a gallows, is dug up using a black dog to avoid its fatal shriek, bringing wealth.

plays.title: Spiritus Familiaris
plays.description: A soldier owns a spirit in a bottle that brings wealth, but he must sell it cheaper to avoid damnation.

plays.title: The Bird's Nest
plays.description: A magical bird's nest is invisible to the eye but visible in water, granting invisibility to its holder.

plays.title: The Breeding Penny
plays.description: A person gets a breeding penny from the devil on Christmas Eve, which later burns down a house in Pantschdorf.

plays.title: The Changeling Beaten with Rods
plays.description: A mother beats a changeling on a Breslau meadow to force the devil to return her real child.

plays.title: Watching Over the Children
plays.description: A Leipzig father watches his newborn child at night to prevent an unseen force from turning its cradle.

plays.title: The Rye Aunt
plays.description: A nursing mother is forced to bind sheaves in the field; the Roggen-Mutter swaps her child but returns it later.

plays.title: The Two Underground Women
plays.description: Two underground women swap a sleeping newborn, but a quick-witted maid saves the child by a clever counter-swap.

plays.title: King Grunewald
plays.description: A princess saves her besieged father by carrying him out on a donkey, disguised by green branches.

plays.title: Blumelisalp
plays.description: A rich shepherd constructs a staircase of cheese and is cursed by his mother, burying the alp under ice.

plays.title: The Lily
plays.description: A nobleman cuts off a servant's head at a banquet but cannot put it back until he kills a hidden disruptor.

plays.title: Johann of Passau
plays.description: A widower’s wife returns from the grave to be his housewife, on the condition that he must never curse again.

plays.title: The Little Dog of Bretta
plays.description: A loyal dog sent to a butcher with a sausage request on a fast day has its tail chopped off in retaliation.

plays.title: The Village by the Sea
plays.description: A proud village mocking a praying woman is buried in sand by oxen digging and a violent storm.

plays.title: The Buried Silver Mines
plays.description: An innocent mine manager cursed a rich mine upon his execution, turning his blood into milk.

plays.title: The Mine Discoverer
plays.description: A heavily indebted miner discovers a rich ore deposit just as he is about to flee, restoring his wealth.

plays.title: A Ghostly Rider
plays.description: An outstanding horse trainer is recognized as a dead soldier, leaves in a jester's outfit, and tricks a groom.

plays.title: The False Oath
plays.description: A peasant swears a false oath to claim land, only for the earth to open and swallow him up.

plays.title: Twelve Unjust Judges
plays.description: Twelve judges in Minden who pass an unjust verdict are punished by the earth swallowing them to their knees.

plays.title: The Holy Springs
plays.description: Three holy springs sprout on Rütli meadow after the Swiss oath, while a traitor burns from within.

plays.title: The Gushing Fountain
plays.description: A fountain dries up whenever a member of a noble Franconian house is dying, but the omen is cheated.

plays.title: Hunger Spring
plays.description: A spring in Halle near the Red Tower predicts cheap or dear times by the flow of its water.

plays.title: The Stream of Love
plays.description: Two lovers dig for forty years to bring a spring to Spangenberg, dying in each other's arms as it succeeds.

plays.title: The Helfenstein
plays.description: A mountain door opens in 1614 for a Marschendorf maid, showing a mystical hall of zeching lords.

plays.title: The Cradle from the Tree
plays.description: A treasure in Rauheneck can only be lifted by a child whose cradle is carved from a tree growing on the tower wall.

plays.title: Hessental
plays.description: A speaking bird warns a Germanic princess of Roman soldiers marching through the valley with gleaming armor.

plays.title: Reinstein
plays.description: Beneath a Harz castle, stones taken from a cave always return, while sounds of bells and blacksmiths echo.

plays.title: The Stationary River
plays.description: The river Fulda ceases its flow as a sign of mourning whenever a member of the princely house of Hesse is about to die.

plays.title: Arendsee
plays.description: A castle sinks to form a deep lake, leaving only Arend and his wife to survive after her historic warning cry.

plays.title: The Ox Hill
plays.description: A noblewoman warned in a dream flees a sinking castle on an ox, watching the ruins sink to form a lake.

plays.title: The Maidens of the Moor
plays.description: Ghostly lights hover over a sunken village in the Red Moor, while a stone bridge remains in the Black Moor.

plays.title: St. Andrew's Eve
plays.description: A girl summons her future husband with dinner on Saint Andrew's Eve, but he suffers pain from the magical compulsion.

plays.title: The Lover Invited to Dinner
plays.description: A woman uses Christmas magic with bread and crossed knives to summon her lover, causing her to flee in terror.

plays.title: Christmas Eve
plays.description: A maid puts a bread crust under her arm by day and under her head by night to dream of her future lover.

plays.title: The Shirt Casting
plays.description: A girl uses a Christmas shirt magic ritual to see her future husband, but a group of girls confuses the spirits.

plays.title: Crystal Gazing
plays.description: A girl uses crystal gazing to discover her future husband, with Johann Rüst witnessing the magical vision.

plays.title: Boiling Magic Herbs
plays.description: A maid cooks magic herbs on Pentecost, forcing a devil in goat form to carry her lover back on its horns.

plays.title: The Salt Journeyman in Pomerania
plays.description: A witch uses magic to pursue a fleeing salt journeyman, sending a black goat to slip between his legs.

plays.title: Maiden Eli
plays.description: A cruel housekeeper haunts Frekenhorst abbey until priests banish her ghost to the Davert forest.

plays.title: The White Lady
plays.description: A white lady combs horses in a stable but is blind inside her dwelling and sees clearly only outside.

plays.title: Dove Reveals a Treasure
plays.description: Duke Heinrich of Breslau follows a dove in Krakow cathedral and finds a hidden treasure under the floorboards.

plays.title: Dove Keeps the Enemy Away
plays.description: A dove prevents a banneret from firing a cannon during a siege, saving the city of Hoxter until Swedish forces arrive.

plays.title: The Bell Casting of Breslau
plays.description: A bell founder kills his apprentice for opening the tap early, only to find the bell is perfect before his execution.

plays.title: The Bell Casting of Attendorn
plays.description: A mother hides a black painted gold plate under a bench until a bell founder discovers and melts it into a new bell.

plays.title: The Miller's Wife
plays.description: A miller's wife traps one robber in a chamber and kills another in the waterwheel while her husband is away at church.

plays.title: Johann Huebner
plays.description: The one-eyed robber Johann Huebner terrorizes a Westphalian forest from his castle until he is killed in battle.

plays.title: Eppela Gaila
plays.description: The robber knight Eppela Gaila Gailingen uses his magical horse to escape from the Nuremberg forces.

plays.title: The Blumenstein
plays.description: A brave village girl wins a midnight bet at a ruined castle and accidentally retrieves a robber's stolen treasure.

plays.title: Seeburger Lake
plays.description: The wicked lifestyle of Count Isang causes his castle to sink, creating the Seeburger lake near Goettingen.

plays.title: The Castle Lake and Wall
plays.description: Priests on the island of Ruegen sacrifice young women to the devil by drowning them in the dark Burgsee.

plays.title: Saint Nicholas and the Thief
plays.description: A thief challenges Saint Nicholas to a race for the offering box but gets carried away by the devil after his death.

plays.title: Giant Stones
plays.description: Giant stones with handprints are found near Leipzig and Salzwedel, where a commander once split a rock with his sword.

plays.title: Imprints in the Stone
plays.description: A retreating commander sits on a stone in Geismarwald and leaves hand and leg imprints as a sign of future victory.

plays.title: The Giant's Finger
plays.description: A wicked giant who mistreated his mother is buried by a storm, leaving his finger protruding as the Fuchsturm tower.

plays.title: Giants from the Untersberg
plays.description: Giants descend from the Untersberg mountain to warn Feldkirchen villagers to live a righteous life.

plays.title: Jetta's Hill in Heidelberg
plays.description: The famous prophetess Jetta predicts the future from her chapel on the hill where Heidelberg Castle stands today.

plays.title: The Giant Haym
plays.description: The giant Haym slays a dragon in the Inntal valley, builds Wilten Abbey, and erects the bridge at Innsbruck.

plays.title: The Dripping Rib
plays.description: A giant pagan maiden's rib drips once a year in Oberburg church, with the final drop signaling Judgment Day.

plays.title: The Maiden's Leap
plays.description: A pure country girl leaps across the Mur river from a mountain to escape a wicked pursuer, naming the cliff.

plays.title: The Bull Brook
plays.description: A shepherd's baptized lamb turns into a terrifying specter that is eventually defeated by a giant milk-fed bull.

plays.title: The Men in Zottenberg
plays.description: A man discovers a hidden cave inside Zottenberg mountain where three silent old robbers await Judgment Day.

plays.title: The Announcement of Doom
plays.description: An old gray-haired man with a youthful face warns the soldiers of Magdeburg of their impending defeat in battle.

plays.title: The Little Man on the Back
plays.description: A rope-maker kicks a boy's game board and is forced by the boy's father to carry him, dying ten days later.

plays.title: Gottschee
plays.description: A hunter is led into a subterranean cave by a spirit and witnesses the silent souls of former castle lords.

plays.title: The Dwarves on the Tree
plays.description: Helpful dwarves who sit on a maple branch during haymaking are betrayed when locals saw it through at night.

plays.title: The Dwarves on the Boulder
plays.description: Dwarves who watch haymakers from a boulder are cruelly burned when pranksters heat the stone red-hot.

plays.title: The Feet of the Dwarves
plays.description: A shepherd scatters ash to discover the dwarves have goose feet, and his mockery drives them away forever.

plays.title: The Wild Spirits
plays.description: German villagers in Veneto appease the Wild Man and Forest Woman with burnt hair and read spirit footprints in ash.

plays.title: The Heiling Dwarves
plays.description: A berry-picking woman enters the dwarf-prince Heiling's cave and awakens a century later to find her world gone.

plays.title: Departure of the Dwarves over the Bridge
plays.description: Dwarves forced to leave their Harz caves agree to cross a bridge in secret while paying a silent wealth tax.

plays.title: The Departure of the Dwarves over the Mountain
plays.description: A baker catches invisible dwarves stealing bread, forcing their population to emigrate and leave a pot of coins.

plays.title: The Dwarves at Dardesheim
plays.description: Helpful dwarves of Dardesheim borrow wedding dishes to locals until human mockery forces them to emigrate forever.

plays.title: Smith Riechert
plays.description: A smith accidentally cracks a dwarf's skull with a threshing flail after guarding his pea field against theft.

plays.title: Grinken-Smith
plays.description: A legendary subterranean smith kills a farmer's horse after the farmer refuses to pay him for borrowing a spit.

plays.title: The Shepherd Boys
plays.description: Two shepherd boys find a hoard of coins inside a mountain cave but barely escape a magically closing stone door.

plays.title: The Nut Kernels
plays.description: A lazy boy following a mysterious nut-cracking lady discovers an underground cellar filled with barrels of gold.

plays.title: The Treasure of Soest
plays.description: A girl follows a goat into a ruin where a maiden gives her cherries that instantly turn into valuable coins.

plays.title: The Gushing Silver
plays.description: A poor girl meeting a white-dressed man in the forest receives a basket of silver coins gushing from a hill.

plays.title: Gold Sand on the Unterberg
plays.description: Men discover gold sand on the Untersberg mountain but face warnings and a fleeting door into another world.

plays.title: Gold Coals
plays.description: An herb gatherer on the Untersberg mountain finds gold-rich gray coals but can never rediscover the stone wall.

plays.title: The Well at Steinau
plays.description: Conspirators murder Abbot Berold of Fulda, leading to their execution and a barren patch of grass near Steinau.

plays.title: The Five Crosses
plays.description: Five ancient stone crosses standing outside the Klaus Gate in Höxter recall legends of giants and hung citizens.

plays.title: The Sword Dance at Weissenstein
plays.description: Peasants secretly arm themselves and trick robber knights by entering Weißenstein castle under the guise of a dance.

plays.title: The Stone Table at Bingenheim
plays.description: A linden tree court in Bingenheim features a stone table left by forest-dwelling giants who left handprints behind.

plays.title: The Tall Man in Murder Lane
plays.description: A giant black man straddling a lane in Hof predicts the coming of the plague before vanishing with a great crash.

plays.title: War and Peace
plays.description: Saxon soldiers capture a hairy wild woman who predicts peace, matching a wild man who foretold war years before.

plays.title: Rodenstein's Departure
plays.description: The ghost of the Lord of Rodenstein noisily marches to Schnellerts castle when war looms and returns in silence.

plays.title: Tannhaeuser
plays.description: Knight Tannhäuser leaves Rome in despair when Pope Urban declares his sins unforgiven unless a dry staff sprouts.

plays.title: The Wild Hunter Hackelberg
plays.description: The ghost of Jägermeister Hackelberg hunts forever on the Solling mountain after refusing heaven for his passion.

plays.title: The Wild Hunter and the Tailor
plays.description: A tailor in Münster mocks the wild hunter and gets knocked unconscious by a ghostly horse foot flying in.

plays.title: The Hoselberg
plays.description: A Thuringian mountain contains purgatory, prompting an English queen to build a chapel at its base to pray.

plays.title: The Rechenberger's Servant
plays.description: Hans von Rechenberg has a loyal servant who performs supernatural feats and is revealed to be a helpful spirit.

plays.title: Ghost Church
plays.description: A woman wanders into a midnight mass of headless spirits who chase her and tear her coat into pieces.

plays.title: The Ghostly Banquet
plays.description: A traveler in a haunted room witnesses a ghostly feast that vanishes upon prayer, leaving behind valuable silver.

plays.title: The Roofer
plays.description: A young roofer speaks irrationally from a tower top before falling to his death while his father prays for him.

plays.title: The Spinner at the Cross
plays.description: A poor woman spins yarn diligently for years to earn enough money to build a stone shrine near Vienna.

plays.title: The Buttermilk Tower
plays.description: Prussian peasants who mock a Deutschmeister are forced to eat a whole barrel of buttermilk inside a tower.

plays.title: Saint Winfried
plays.description: Saint Boniface stamps a foot imprint into the Christenberg and causes a dry wooden staff to sprout green leaves.

plays.title: The Huelfenberg
plays.description: A tricked devil bursts out of a chapel on the Hülfenberg, leaving a hole in the gable that can never be closed.

plays.title: The Devil's Hole in Goslar
plays.description: A violent dispute in Goslar is incensed by the devil, leaving a hole in the church wall that requires a cat to seal.

plays.title: The Devil's Mill
plays.description: A miller makes a pact with the devil to build a windmill, but a premature rooster crow causes its destruction.

plays.title: The Footprint of the Lord
plays.description: Christ leaves footprint imprints in rock after banishing the devil to a ravine to weep tears for a thousand years.

plays.title: The Sachsenhausen Bridge in Frankfurt
plays.description: A builder tricks the devil by driving a rooster across a newly completed bridge built under a soul contract.

plays.title: The Wolf and the Pinecone
plays.description: Aachen citizens trick the devil by sending a wolf into the cathedral first, causing him to slam and split the door.

plays.title: The Devil of Ach
plays.description: The devil is banished inside a large tower in the city wall of Aachen, where he makes noise until Judgment Day.

plays.title: The Devil's Wall
plays.description: A premature rooster crow ruins a pact between God and the devil, who breaks his own wall in a furious rage.

plays.title: The Devil's Dance Floor
plays.description: The devil builds walls to divide the Earth with God and dances in celebration on a rocky plateau in the Harz.

plays.title: The Devil's Pulpit
plays.description: The devil once preached to the local people from a row of rocks in Baden called the Devil's Pulpit.

plays.title: The Devil's Ear Pillow
plays.description: A flat stone at the foot of Castle Bentheim contains the ear print of the devil who once slept upon it.

plays.title: The Devil's Rock
plays.description: Satan tempts Christ from the Cößein rock but excludes two rough villages which remain the devil's own territory.

plays.title: Devil's Wall
plays.description: The devil tries to build a stone wall across the Danube, but the stones constantly slip out of his hands.

plays.title: The Devil's Grille
plays.description: A frustrated blacksmith wishes the devil to finish an intricate baptismal grid, and the devil immediately does so.

plays.title: The Devil's Mill
plays.description: The devil builds a Weser windmill and drives the wheel with a rocky stream, creating a local legend.

plays.title: The Devil's Church
plays.description: The devil steals building stones from a valley church project and piles them up on a Rhön mountain peak.

plays.title: The Devil's Stone near Reichenbach
plays.description: The devil builds a complex stone shelter in a single night to house local villagers during war times.

plays.title: The Devil's Stone near Cologne
plays.description: The devil attempts to destroy a chapel in Cologne by throwing a heavy stone but fails completely.

plays.title: The Suentelstein near Osnabrueck
plays.description: The devil carries a massive stone through the air by a chain and drops it near the city of Osnabrück.

plays.title: The Stone of Lies
plays.description: The devil agrees to drop a rock next to the Halberstadt Dom in exchange for a promised wine house.

plays.title: The Rock Bridge
plays.description: A shepherd leaps over a heap of coals placed by the devil on a narrow bridge after praying to the Virgin Mary.

plays.title: The Devil's Pool near Dassel
plays.description: The devil steals a golden bell and later drags an impious plowing farmer down into the bottomless Teufelsbad.

plays.title: The Tower of Schartfeld
plays.description: The devil throws the roof off the Schartfeld tower to expose a monk who abetted the emperor's adultery.

plays.title: Cologne Cathedral
plays.description: Cologne Cathedral remains unfinished after its proud builder loses a bet to the devil and leaps to his death.

plays.title: The Devil's Hat
plays.description: The devil boasts of wearing a massive stone as a hat until Christ easily lifts it with a single finger.

plays.title: The Devil's Fire
plays.description: The devil mockingly exposes the sins of local priests and burns down the town of Schiltach using a magic kettle.

plays.title: The Devil's Horseshoes
plays.description: The devil rides a cheating beer seller to a blacksmith who delays the shoeing until the morning cock crows.

plays.title: The Devil Carries the Bride Away
plays.description: A rich woman breaks her wedding vow to her poor lover and is carried off by the devil during her wedding dance.

plays.title: The Wheel of Fortune
plays.description: Twelve soldiers spin on the devil's wheel of fortune where one is taken and the rest wake up empty-handed.

plays.title: The Devil as Advocate
plays.description: The devil disguised in a blue hat defends a cheated mercenary and carries off the swearing lying landlord.

plays.title: Dream of the Treasure on the Bridge
plays.description: A dreamer finds a treasure under a tree after a merchant on a bridge mocks dreams and tells him where to dig.

plays.title: The Kettle with the Treasure
plays.description: A rising kettle of gold sinks back under the stove when a girl cries out in surprise instead of keeping silent.

plays.title: The Werewolf
plays.description: A logger transforms into a werewolf using a magic belt and devours a foal while his companion secretly watches.

plays.title: The Werewolf Stone
plays.description: A shepherd chases a mocking werewolf assistant who transforms into a thornbush before being struck down.

plays.title: The Werewolves Set Out
plays.description: A leader with a wire whip drives a horde of shape-shifted werewolves across parted waters to raid Livonian herds.

plays.title: The Dragon Flies Out
plays.description: A Swiss cooper survives the winter inside a deep pit with two dragons and escapes by holding onto a dragon's tail.

plays.title: Winkelried and the Dragon
plays.description: An exiled hero kills a devastating dragon using thorns but dies from the monster's toxic blood on his skin.

plays.title: The Dragon at the Well
plays.description: A knight defeats a well-guarding dragon but is killed when the monster's venomous tail stings his unarmored knee.

plays.title: The Dragon's Cave
plays.description: A count slays a giant dragon and retrieves his younger brother alive from the split belly of the monster.

plays.title: The Snake Queen
plays.description: A snake queen rewards a kind shepherd girl with a golden crown on her wedding day to bring her eternal wealth.

plays.title: The Virgin of Oselberg
plays.description: A cursed castle maiden appears as a ghost snake with a human head and key ring during the quarterly Ember days.

plays.title: The Toad Stool
plays.description: A proud cursed daughter appearing as a snake or toad must be kissed weekly on Nothweiler Castle to be redeemed.

plays.title: The Meadow Maiden
plays.description: A fearful shepherd boy thrice refuses to rescue a white meadow maiden who is cursed to wait for a cherrywood cradle.

plays.title: The Sneezing in the Water
plays.description: A spirit sneezing in the Auerbach is freed when a traveler blesses him but a curse falls on a cursing traveler.

plays.title: The Poor Soul
plays.description: A sneezing soul under a Paderborn bridge tips over any passing carriage whose driver fails to say a blessing.

plays.title: The Cursed Maiden
plays.description: A stubborn daughter cursed to dwell in an Eisenach cave can only be saved if a traveler blesses her when she sneezes.

plays.title: The Maiden of Staufenberg
plays.description: An enchanted castle maiden with golden curly hair periodically appears on a Harz mountain cliff bearing her footprint.

plays.title: The Virgin's Stone
plays.description: A daughter who gathers blueberries on Sunday instead of attending church is cursed by her mother and turns to stone.

plays.title: The Stone Bridal Bed
plays.description: A mother curses her disobedient daughter's bridal bed which turns to stone on her wedding night during a storm.

plays.title: Cursed to Stand
plays.description: An angry father curses his lazy son to stand in one spot for seven years wearing deep holes into the floorboards.

plays.title: The Dancers of Kolbeck
plays.description: Peasants mocking Christmas mass are cursed by a priest to dance for a whole year without stopping for food or rest.

plays.title: The Holy Sunday
plays.description: Spinnings on Sunday bring fire that consumes a home while a Sunday plowman has his tool grow directly into his hand.

plays.title: Lady Huett
plays.description: A giant queen is turned to stone after her servant abuses bread crumbs to wipe mud off her young son.

plays.title: The Kindelsberg
plays.description: Overproud knights are struck by the plague before a beautiful daughter is slain by a rejected black knight.

plays.title: The Roll Shoes
plays.description: A vain daughter who wears hollowed-out rolls as shoes to church sinks with her castle into the ground.

plays.title: The Sinkhole near Hochstaedt
plays.description: Angry boys who stomp and whip their bread are swallowed up by a sudden sinkhole while the innocent boy escapes.

plays.title: The Bread Shoes
plays.description: A grieving mother buries her child in shoes made of bread, whose spirit haunts her until they are replaced.

plays.title: The Deaf Grain
plays.description: A rich widow who dumps grain into the sea has her harbor blocked by a sandbank where only deaf grass grows.

plays.title: The Lady's Sand
plays.description: A proud maiden who wastes grain is ruined after a swallowed ring predicted her demise from starvation.

plays.title: Bread Turned to Stone
plays.description: Greedy and heartless people who refuse bread to the needy have their loaves miraculously turned to stone.

plays.title: The Mouse Tower of Bingen
plays.description: A cruel archbishop who burns starving peasants in a barn is chased and eaten alive by mice in a Rhine tower.

plays.title: The Boys' Clearing
plays.description: Two begging boys who refuse to share a small loaf of bread fight and beat each other to death with fence stakes.

plays.title: Children's Bridge
plays.description: A Thuringian town is named after two children who ride hobbyhorses on a bridge and drown in the river below.

plays.title: The Children of Hameln
plays.description: A strange piper lures Hamelin's rats into a river and later leads all the children away when citizens refuse pay.

plays.title: The Rat Catcher
plays.description: A miller takes a magic cane left by a rat catcher, causing all the rats in the village to follow him to his mill.

plays.title: The Snake Catcher
plays.description: A Salzburg sorcerer who boasts he can gather and destroy all snakes is coiled and dragged to his death by a giant one.

plays.title: The Little Mouse
plays.description: A sleeping maid dies after a curious bystander moves her body, preventing her soul mouse from returning.

plays.title: The Departing Smoke
plays.description: A master watches his maids send black smoke to press a stableboy and dies after shaking one during her sleep.

plays.title: The Cat from the Willow Tree
plays.description: Farmhands follow a maid who turns into a cat in a hollow willow tree, leaving her body lifeless until she returns.

plays.title: Making Weather and Hail
plays.description: Witches who attempt to freeze crops or call down hail are punished by local citizens or fail in their magic.

plays.title: The Witches' Dance
plays.description: A whistling boy who climbs a tree to play for a witch gathering invokes God's name, causing them all to vanish.

plays.title: The Grapevines and Noses
plays.description: A court illusionist tricks his dinner guests into holding their own noses with knives, believing they cut grapes.

plays.title: Hanging Fast
plays.description: A Magdeburg crowd watches a magician and his household climb a rope into the sky, while he walks back to his inn.

plays.title: The Emergency Shirt
plays.description: A magic linen shirt spun by two innocent young girls on Christmas Eve protects its wearer from all battle wounds.

plays.title: Made Bulletproof
plays.description: Soldiers who know the art of invulnerability ward off all bullets or shake them unharmed from their sleeves.

plays.title: The Sure Shot
plays.description: A magic marksman is able to hit any named target within range, even if he fires at random through a window.

plays.title: The Wandering Hunter
plays.description: A mysterious hunter puts an end to a series of phantom sniper killings in a haunted forest.

plays.title: Double Form
plays.description: A sick nobleman is tricked by a traveler into accusing his honest neighbor of witchcraft through a phantom double.

plays.title: The Spectre Wife
plays.description: A nobleman is plagued by a phantom that perfectly mirrors his wife and follows them through every house.

plays.title: Death of the Firstborn
plays.description: An innocent nurse is executed for suffocating a baby and leaves behind a curse on the family's firstborn sons.

plays.title: The Boy of Colmar
plays.description: A clairvoyant boy senses a hidden skeleton in a garden and slowly wastes away from his terrifying visions.

plays.title: Death of the Canon of Merseburg
plays.description: A mysterious thunderous blow strikes a canon's seat in Merseburg Cathedral three weeks before his death.

plays.title: The Lily in the Monastery of Corvey
plays.description: A warning lily descending onto a monk's seat in Corvey Abbey leads to the sudden death of a young mock monk.

plays.title: Rebundus in the Cathedral of Lubeck
plays.description: A Lubeck canon tries to pass a death warning rose to his neighbor, resulting in a curse that shakes his tomb.

plays.title: The Bell Tolls by Itself
plays.description: Self-tolling church and house bells warn citizens of a town of their impending death or that of their loved ones.

plays.title: The Spectre of Death
plays.description: A shapeshifting spectre in Tyrol peers through windows to signal the imminent death of those residing within.

plays.title: Frau Berta or the White Lady
plays.description: The ghostly White Lady of Neuhaus signals the imminent death or joy of several noble families she visits.

plays.title: The Wild Berta is Coming
plays.description: The terrifying wild Berta punishes girls who fail to spin their flax by cutting open their bellies.

plays.title: The Tuerst, the Posterli and the Straeggele
plays.description: Lucerne forest storms and winter solstice spirits chase girls who have not finished their daily spinning work.

plays.title: The Night Hunter and the Ruettelweiber
plays.description: The mythical Night Hunter of the Giant Mountains pursues small moss-clothed spirits fleeing through the woods.

plays.title: The Man with the Slouch Hat
plays.description: A bearded ghost in a slouch hat visits an old woman in a ruined castle and silently smokes a pipe at her table.

plays.title: The Gray Hockelmann
plays.description: A grey ghost forces an Auerbach farmer to carry him up to the castle hill, leading to the farmer's fatal exhaustion.

plays.title: Chimmeke in Pomerania
plays.description: The Pomeranian house spirit Chimmeke chops up a kitchen boy in Schloss Lonz for eating his daily milk offering.

plays.title: The Screecher
plays.description: A shapeshifting forest ghost mimicking animal cries terrifies local shepherds and leads travelers astray.

plays.title: The Ferrying Monks
plays.description: Ghostly monks cross the Rhine in Speyer, beating a fisherman during a storm before flying away in a fiery cart.

plays.title: The Will-o'-the-Wisp
plays.description: A will-o-the-wisp follows a mocking girl into her parents' home and beats the family with its fiery wings.

plays.title: The Fiery Carriage
plays.description: A watchman in Odenwald encounters two horrific fiery carriages carrying ghosts with flames shooting from their mouths.

plays.title: The Raederberg
plays.description: A Nassau butcher enters a ghostly mansion, drinks a third cup from three monks, and dies three days later.

plays.title: The Lights on Halberds
plays.description: Brushing a warning light off a pitchfork three times results in a fatal duel between two close farmer friends.

plays.title: The Wafeln
plays.description: Baltic Sea people witness glowing apparitions of ships, houses, and people warning of their impending doom.

plays.title: The Hovering Castle of Flames
plays.description: A brave Tyrolean nobleman climbs a cursed mountain to explore a fiery castle and is abducted by a black rider.

plays.title: The Fire Mountain
plays.description: A guide leads a shepherd into the fiery Feuerberg where a deceased debtor count hands him a proof of his damnation.

plays.title: The Fiery Man
plays.description: A transparent fiery man walking between boundary stones and spitting fire from his mouth terrifies the local villagers.

plays.title: The Cursed Land Surveyors
plays.description: Deceitful land surveyors are cursed to wander after death as will-o-the-wisps guarding the borders they manipulated.

plays.title: The Displaced Boundary Stone
plays.description: The ghost of a fraudulent nobleman touches a girl on her breast, turning it black and causing her death in three days.

plays.title: The Boundary Dispute
plays.description: Villagers resolve a land border dispute by tracking a crawfish walking across a field to place their boundary stones.

plays.title: The Boundary Run
plays.description: Uri and Glarus runners compete in a boundary race started by rooster crows, ending in a Glarner runner carrying Uri.

plays.title: The Battle of the Alp
plays.description: Entlebuch shepherds silently steal Obwalden cattle by removing their cowbells and win the subsequent armed conflict.

plays.title: The Stone near Wenthusen
plays.description: A cursed monastery stone brings severe misfortune to any owner who dares to move it from its designated place.

plays.title: The Altenberg Church
plays.description: Altenberg villagers fail to move a mountaintop church down to the valley as its stones return uphill every night.

plays.title: The King in the Lauenburg Mountain
plays.description: Two condemned criminals descend into a Kashubian abyss and find a hidden garden and a king sitting on a silver chair.

plays.title: The Schwanberg
plays.description: An ancient German prophecy foretells that Germany will eventually be absorbed entirely into Switzerland.

plays.title: The Well of Robbedissen
plays.description: A spring, a black ground, and a giant poplar tree warn local villagers of an impending bloody battle in the field.

plays.title: The Bamberg Scales
plays.description: A rumor warns that when the tilted tongue of the scale of Justice in Bamberg becomes balanced, the world will end.

plays.title: Emperor Frederick at Kaiserslautern
plays.description: A man let down on a rope into a deep cave encounters Emperor Frederick sitting on a golden throne with his knights.

plays.title: The Shepherd on the Kyffhaeuser
plays.description: A Thuringian shepherd plays his bagpipe for Emperor Frederick on a mountain and is rewarded with a golden vessel leg.

plays.title: The Three Tells
plays.description: Three legendary Swiss liberators sleep in a hidden cave, briefly waking to ask a shepherd if the time has come.

plays.title: The Mountain Dwarf
plays.description: Swiss mountain dwarves reward a plowing shepherd and his servant with a tablecloth and a plate of fresh cake.

plays.title: The Swiss Stone Pine Nuts
plays.description: A master curses the sweet Swiss pine nut trees to grow only on precipitous cliffs, punishing lazy servants.

plays.title: The Paradise of the Animals
plays.description: A hidden valley high on the Mattenberg serves as a sanctuary for animals, accessible only once every twenty years.

plays.title: The Chamois Hunter
plays.description: A mountain dwarf promises a hunter weekly chamois meat, but kills him when the hunter breaks the pact to hunt again.

plays.title: The Dwarf Holes
plays.description: Helpful Harz dwarves lend wedding kitchen utensils to local villagers from their rock cave, demanding timely returns.

plays.title: The Dwarf and the Magic Flower
plays.description: A shepherd finds a magic flower on the Kyffhäuser, enters a vault of gold, but loses the flower forever.

plays.title: The Nix at the Kelle
plays.description: A midwife delivers a water sprite's baby in a lake cave but later sees a bloody stream representing the child's murder.

plays.title: Schwarzach
plays.description: A knight tastes a forbidden water drink and sinks into a lake where his daughter is held captive by a giant snake.

plays.title: The Three Maidens from the Lake
plays.description: A youth sets the village clock back to keep three lake maidens longer, leading to their tragic deaths in the water.

plays.title: The Dead Bridegroom
plays.description: A magician offers to rescue a drowned bridegroom from a Magdeburg nix in exchange for the couple's souls.

plays.title: The Eternal Hunter
plays.description: Graf Eberhard encounters a lord cursed to hunt the same stag for centuries with a face dried up like a sponge.

plays.title: Hans Jagenteufel
plays.description: A Dresden woman meets a headless horseman holding his own head who warns her to repent of her sins.

plays.title: Hackelnberg's Dream
plays.description: A Brunswick master hunter kills a boar from his prophetic dream but dies from a wound caused by kicking its tusk.

plays.title: The Screech Owl
plays.description: A howling Thuringian nun is banished as an owl to the Harz where she joins the wild hunt of Hackelnberg.

plays.title: The Black Riders and the Led Horse
plays.description: A robber knight's servant learns from a black rider of the Wild Hunt that he will die and ride their empty horse.

plays.title: The Faithful Eckhart
plays.description: The Faithful Eckhart leads the spectral Wild Hunt on Fat Tuesday, warning onlookers to clear the way for safety.

plays.title: The Lady of Willberg
plays.description: A man uses a magic bouquet to enter a mountain cave of gold but forgets it, turning his treasures to paper.

plays.title: The Shepherd and the Old Man from the Mountain
plays.description: An old man shows a shepherd a cave of treasures that remains open only if the mortal leaves his gloves inside.

plays.title: Maiden Ilse
plays.description: A cursed princess of Ilsenstein gives a charcoal burner a satchel of gold disguised as acorns and pinecones.

plays.title: The Pagan Maiden of Glatz
plays.description: A Silesian pagan princess with magical strength is walled up after committing incest and haunts those who mock her.

plays.title: The Rosstrapp and the Cretpfuhl
plays.description: A princess leaps across a Harz abyss to escape a giant, leaving a hoofprint and dropping her golden crown.

plays.title: The Maiden's Leap
plays.description: A giantess scoops up a ploughman and leaps across the Selke Valley, leaving her footprints pressed into the rock.

plays.title: The Maiden's Leap
plays.description: A maiden flees a lustful monk at Oybin monastery and leaps off a cliff, saved from injury by descending angels.

plays.title: Harras's Leap
plays.description: A knight leaps off a steep cliff into the Zschopau valley to escape enemies, surviving while his horse is killed.

plays.title: The Giant Hidde
plays.description: A giant forest warden presents lion cubs as wolves to his duke and is rewarded with lands that become Hitzacker.

plays.title: The Ilfeld Needle-Eye
plays.description: A giant drops a stone that pinched his shoe, which becomes a narrow tunnel used for stableboy initiation rites.

plays.title: The Giants at Lichtenberg
plays.description: A giantess scoops up a human farmer with his plough and oxen to show her husband at Castle Lichtenberg.

plays.title: The Giant's Blood
plays.description: A giant jumping over Westeregeln nicks his toe on a church spire, creating a red pool of giant blood.

plays.title: Rustling in the Giant's Grave
plays.description: Stonecutters digging up giant graves in Pomerania flee after hearing a spooky rustling of keys around them.

plays.title: Dead Men from the Graves Fight off the Enemy
plays.description: Dead ancestors rise from their graves in Wehrstedt to defend their children from a pagan invasion.

plays.title: Hans Heiling's Rocks
plays.description: Hans Heiling makes a pact with the devil to turn a wedding party to stone after the bride rejects him.

plays.title: The Virgin with the Beard
plays.description: A princess grows a beard to protect her vow of chastity, is crucified, and drops her slipper to a minstrel.

plays.title: The White Virgin of Schwanau
plays.description: The ghost of a disgraced girl pursues a cruel Vogt around Castle Schwanau until he drowns in the lake.

plays.title: Schwarzkopf and Seeburg at Lake Mummel
plays.description: Twelve sisters avenge their brother by killing twelve robber barons, leading to their tragic end in Mummelsee.

plays.title: The Peddler and the Mouse
plays.description: A kind peddler sharing bread with a mouse is rewarded with gold coins, leading to the construction of a church.

plays.title: The Three Treasure Diggers
plays.description: Three craftsmen attempting to dig up a church treasure near Erfurt are killed by the guarding evil spirit.

plays.title: Invitation Before God's Court
plays.description: A nobleman mockingly invites hanged thieves to dinner, leading to a chilling prophecy and his own execution.

plays.title: Guests from the Gallows
plays.description: An innkeeper drunkenly invites three hanged men to dine, receives a terrifying visit, and dies three days later.

plays.title: Devil's Bridge
plays.description: A shepherd tricks the devil into building a bridge across the Reuss by sending a chamois across it first.

plays.title: The Twelve Johns
plays.description: The devil takes one of the king's twelve Johns every year as a toll from their flying magic disk.

plays.title: Devil's Ditch
plays.description: A farmer accepts the devil's help to dig a ditch, but the seven helper workers pay with their lives.

plays.title: The Kreuzliberg
plays.description: A princess is turned to stone by mountain spirits but is later redeemed by a holy image.

plays.title: The Horses from the Loft Hole
plays.description: A mayor's resurrected wife is let inside only after his white horses miraculously climb to the hayloft.

plays.title: Meeting of the Dead
plays.description: A captain witnesses the ghost of a dying mistress of the robes visiting a deceased queen at midnight.

plays.title: The Prophetic Bird
plays.description: A tiny white bird on an oak tree gives Barbara Sellentius a prophecy for the Duke of Pomerania.

plays.title: The Wandering Jew on the Matterhorn
plays.description: The Wandering Jew visits a city on the Matterberg and foretells its transformation into snow and ice.

plays.title: The Kettle of Butter
plays.description: A selfish woman refuses to share butter with God in disguise, leading to the burial of her entire village.

plays.title: The Weeping Willow
plays.description: The weeping willow hangs its branches to the earth in eternal mourning for being used to scourge Christ.

plays.title: The Christ Image at Wittenberg
plays.description: A miraculous image of Christ in Wittenberg always appears exactly one inch taller than the viewer.

plays.title: The Mother of God Image on the Cliff
plays.description: A desecrated image of Mary flees to a high cliff and turns rescue ropes as thin as hair to prevent removal.

plays.title: The Miraculous Image from the Larch Trunk at Waldrast
plays.description: An angel commands a larch trunk to grow an image of Mary, and a woodcutter is chosen by a voice to build a chapel.

plays.title: Oxen Show the Holy Site
plays.description: A community uses yoked oxen to locate the divinely chosen site for building Saint Stephen church.

plays.title: Notburga
plays.description: Princess Notburga escapes a forced marriage by riding a white stag across the Neckar to live in a cave.

plays.title: Mortar Slaked with Wine
plays.description: Emperor Friedrich the Third orders citizens to use extremely sour wine to slake mortar for building Saint Stephen.

plays.title: The Jew's Stone
plays.description: A poor father sells his child to murderers for money that turns into dry leaves after the child is killed.

plays.title: The Little Girl Killed by the Jews
plays.description: A murdered girl raises her hand from a river to expose her killers, whose guilt is proven as her wounds bleed.

plays.title: The Four Horseshoes
plays.description: Count Ernst of Klettenberg rides his horse into a church during vespers, causing the horseshoes to fall off.

plays.title: The Altar at Seefeld
plays.description: A proud knight sinks into the church floor after demanding a larger host, while roses bloom from a dry stock.

plays.title: The Stone of Dying
plays.description: A victim of a murder in the Kirchetbuel gorge leaves a permanent handprint pressed into a solid stone.

plays.title: Sinful Love
plays.description: A brother and sister commit incest and are decapitated by the devil, leaving a headless mark on their gravestone.

plays.title: The Councilor of Schweidnitz
plays.description: A greedy councilor trains a jackdaw to steal coins from the treasury and dies starving on a high tower ledge.

plays.title: Rainbow over the Condemned
plays.description: A double rainbow appears over Prague to comfort twenty-seven men condemned to death for their part in a revolt.

plays.title: God Weeps with the Innocent
plays.description: A rain shower falls from a clear blue sky to witness the innocence of an accused woman at her execution site.

plays.title: God's Food
plays.description: A boy lost in a snowstorm is protected from the cold and fed by a mysterious messenger sent from heaven.

plays.title: The Three Old Men
plays.description: A priest is shown three extremely ancient men, each older than the last, who live in a state of holy waiting.

plays.title: The Holy Salt River
plays.description: Germanic tribes fight for a salt-rich river, believing its divine harvest connects them directly to the gods.

plays.title: The Holy Lake of Hertha
plays.description: Slaves washing the sacred wagon of the goddess Hertha are swallowed by the lake to protect her holy secrets.

plays.title: The Sacred Forest of the Semnones
plays.description: Semnones perform a human sacrifice in a sacred grove where everyone must enter bound in chains of submission.

plays.title: The Migration of the Ampsivarii
plays.description: An exiled Germanic tribe seeking a home is repeatedly driven away until all their youth are wiped out.

plays.title: The Sea Voyage of the Usipii
plays.description: Mutinous Germanic conscripts flee Britain in stolen ships, resort to cannibalism, and are sold as slaves.

plays.title: Migration of the Goths
plays.description: The Goths migrate under their first kings, but a collapsed bridge permanently divides their army during migration.

plays.title: The Collapsed Bridge
plays.description: After a bridge collapses, half of the migrating Goths are isolated in an unreachable swamp where their echoes persist.

plays.title: Why the Goths Invaded Greece
plays.description: The removal of three buried silver statues guarding the northern border triggers a barbarian invasion of Thrace.

plays.title: Fridigern
plays.description: Famine-stricken Goths sell their children for dog meat to Roman exploiters, leading to a historic rebellion.

plays.title: The King's Grave
plays.description: The Goths divert a river to bury King Alaric with his treasures, executing the diggers to keep it secret.

plays.title: Athaulf's Death
plays.description: A Vandal king is assassinated in Spain, either for mocking a short man or due to a servant's ancient blood feud.

plays.title: The Trulls
plays.description: Starving Goths are mocked by Vandals with the derogatory name Truller after buying overpriced grain.

plays.title: Saga of Gelimer
plays.description: An accidental arrow hit from a drunk bodyguard kills a commander, allowing Vandal King Gelimer to escape.

plays.title: Gelimer in Silver Chains
plays.description: Vandal King Gelimer is outwitted with a silver chain by Belisarius and proves his courage in a combat trial.

plays.title: Origin of the Huns
plays.description: Banished Gothic witches mate with wild forest-spirits in the desert, giving rise to the feral Huns.

plays.title: The Immigration of the Huns
plays.description: A miraculous female deer guides Hun hunters through an impassable swamp, revealing a secret path to Scythia.

plays.title: Saga of the Huns
plays.description: Rumors attribute the Huns' origin to exile, a ransom horse, or a union between a wizard and a she-wolf.

plays.title: The War Sword
plays.description: A shepherd finds a buried sword that wounded a cow and presents it to King Attila as a sign of world dominion.

plays.title: The Storks
plays.description: Attila interprets storks leaving a besieged city as a prophetic sign, motivating his army to sack Aquileia.

plays.title: The Fish on the Table
plays.description: Guilt-ridden King Theodoric mistakes a served fish head for his executed servant Symmachus and falls mortally ill.

plays.title: Theodoric's Soul
plays.description: A holy hermit on Lipari sees a vision of the deceased King Theodoric being thrown into the volcano crater.

plays.title: Urajas and Ildibad
plays.description: A queen's humiliation at the baths leads to a nobleman's murder and subsequent revenge against the king.

plays.title: Totila Tests the Saint
plays.description: Gothic King Totila tests Saint Benedict with a masquerade, but the saint instantly exposes the fake king.

plays.title: The Blind Sabinus
plays.description: The blind Bishop Sabinus detects Totila's identity by touch and later survives a poison attempt by his archdeacon.

plays.title: The Departure of the Lombards
plays.description: Overpopulated Winili draw lots to migrate under Ibor and Ayo, defeating Vandals and settling in Italy.

plays.title: The Departure of the Lombards
plays.description: In a famine-stricken Denmark, Ebbe and Aage's mother suggests casting lots for a third of the Goths to migrate.

plays.title: Saga of Gambara and the Longbeards
plays.description: Led by Gambara, the exiled Goths refuse to pay Vandal tribute and resolve to fight for their freedom.

plays.title: The Lombards and the Assipiters
plays.description: The Goths use a clever dog-headed warrior scare to bypass Assipiter borders, resolved in single combat.

plays.title: The Seven Sleepers in the Cave
plays.description: Seven Roman sleepers in a coastal cave remain incorruptible, protected by a curse against thieves.

plays.title: Lamissio and the Amazons
plays.description: Lamissio wins a swimming duel against an Amazon champion, securing river passage for the Lombards.

plays.title: The Boy in the Fishpond
plays.description: King Agelmund rescues an abandoned boy who grabs his spear in a fishpond, naming him Lamissio.

plays.title: Saga of Rodulf and Rumetrud
plays.description: A princess mocks a short envoy, leading to a fatal insult and his cowardly assassination from behind.

plays.title: Alboin Becomes Table-Worthy to Audoin
plays.description: Alboin is armed by the Gepid king in his slain son's seat, earning the right to speise with his father.

plays.title: Arrival of the Lombards in Italy
plays.description: Mocked by the Empress with a golden spindle, Narses retaliates by inviting the Lombards to invade Italy.

plays.title: Alboin Surveys Italy
plays.description: Alboin climbs a mountain named Königsberg to survey Italy, a land rumored to house giant wisents.

plays.title: Alboin Wins Ticinum
plays.description: Alboin's horse collapses at the gate, rising only after the king breaks a vow to massacre the citizens.

plays.title: Alboin and Rosimund
plays.description: Alboin forces Queen Rosimund to drink from her father's skull cup, driving her to plot his death.

plays.title: Rosimund, Helmichis, and Peredeo
plays.description: After Alboin's murder, Helmichis and Rosimund flee to Ravenna, where they both end up poisoning each other.

plays.title: Authari's Column
plays.description: King Authari rides into the sea at Reggio and strikes a column with his spear to establish the Lombard border.

plays.title: Saga of King Authari
plays.description: King Authari wooes Bavarian princess Theodelind in disguise, revealing his identity with a touch.

plays.title: Agilulf and Theudelind
plays.description: Queen Theodelind chooses Agilulf as king, proposing with a kiss during their meeting at Laumell.

plays.title: Theodelind and the Sea Monster
plays.description: Theodelind is assaulted by a sea monster, giving birth to a dark prince who grows up cruel and malicious.

plays.title: Romhild and Grimoald the Boy
plays.description: Romhild betrays Friuli for love of the Hun king Cacan, who later cruelly executes her for treachery.

plays.title: Leupichis Escapes
plays.description: An escaping captive boy is guided by a wolf and saved from starvation in the wilderness by a prophetic dream.

plays.title: The Fly at the Window
plays.description: A fly chopped of one leg by the king transforms into a one-legged man who warns the targets of the murder plot.

plays.title: King Liutprand's Feet
plays.description: King Liutprand's exceptionally long feet become the standard unit for measuring Goth fields.

plays.title: Aistulf's Birth
plays.description: A king father tests five newborn sons with a spear, sparing and naming the child who grabs the shaft.

plays.title: The Bird on the Spear
plays.description: A cuckoo perching on Hildeprand's coronation spear is interpreted as a sign of a useless and brief reign.

plays.title: Walther in the Cloister
plays.description: Old hero Walter of Aquitaine does penance in a monastery, defending its transports from robbers with a simple stick.

plays.title: Origin of the Saxons
plays.description: The Saxons and King Aschanes grow out of the Harz rocks, and Saxon maidens grow on forest trees.

plays.title: Descent of the Saxons
plays.description: Alexander's veterans land on the Elbe and betray the resident Thuringians using large Saxe knives.

plays.title: Origin of the Saxons in Land Law
plays.description: Alexander's surviving veterans settle in Saxony, sparing local peasants and binding them as serfs.

plays.title: The Saxons and the Thuringians
plays.description: A Saxon youth tricks a Thuringian by exchanging gold chains for land, scattering gold dust to claim a wide territory.

plays.title: Arrival of the Angles and Saxons
plays.description: British King Vortigern invites the Saxons to defend Britain, prompting a massive Saxon migration.

plays.title: Arrival of the Picts
plays.description: The Picts are redirected by the Scots to settle in northern Britain, securing Scottish wives under matrilineal terms.

plays.title: The Saxons Build Ochsenburg
plays.description: The Saxons cut an ox-hide into a thin cord, claiming a large territory to build Ochsenburg fortress.

plays.title: Hatred Between Saxons and Swabians
plays.description: Swabians take Goth wives during their campaign, causing a return war and disinheriting the traitorous women.

plays.title: Origin of the Swabians
plays.description: The Swabians' ancestors land at Mount Suevo and their Duke Brenno fights Julius Caesar.

plays.title: Descent of the Bavarians
plays.description: The Bavarians immigrate from Armenia, forging legendary swords and fighting Julius Caesar.

plays.title: Origin of the Franks
plays.description: Trojans escaping the fall of Troy wander to the Rhine, founding a new Troy called Santen.

plays.title: The Merovingians
plays.description: Queen Clodio is overwhelmed by a sea monster, giving birth to Merovech, the ancestor of the bristly kings.

plays.title: Childeric and Basina
plays.description: An exiled King uses a split gold ring to signal his return, winning the Thuringian Queen Basina.

plays.title: The Vase of Soissons
plays.description: Clovis avenges a broken church vase by slaying the proud warrior who struck it with an axe.

plays.title: Remigius Outlines His Land
plays.description: Remigius walks the borders of his granted land; a stubborn miller's wheel spins backward as punishment.

plays.title: Remigius Expels the Fire
plays.description: Remigius' footprints press into stone as he drives a raging fire out of Rheims and locks the city gate.

plays.title: Remigius' Part of the Vosges Forest
plays.description: A magical boundary stone in a hollow tree cannot be removed, withering the hand of a thief who strikes it.

plays.title: Clotilda's Betrothal
plays.description: Envoy Aurelian disguises as a beggar to slip Clovis' betrothal ring to Burgundian princess Clotilda.

plays.title: The Shears and the Sword
plays.description: A mourning Queen chooses death over shearing for her grandsons; the brutal kings murder the boys.

plays.title: Legend of Attalus and Leo
plays.description: The loyal cook Leo gains a Frankish lord's trust to rescue the noble hostage Attalus on horseback.

plays.title: The Sleeping King
plays.description: King Guntram's soul exits as a snake to find a treasure cave, guided by a servant's sword bridge.

plays.title: The Moving Forest and the Ringing Bells
plays.description: Frankish soldiers carry tree branches and horse bells to trick Childebert's army into routing.

plays.title: Clotaire's Victory over the Saxons
plays.description: Clotaire avenges his wounded son Dagobert by crossing the Weser and slaying Duke Bertoald.

plays.title: The Grave of the Saints
plays.description: Dagobert finds sanctuary in Saint Denis' chapel after shearing a Duke's beard, escaping Clotaire's wrath.

plays.title: Saint Arbogast
plays.description: Saint Arbogast resurrects King Dagobert's son Sigebert after he is trampled to death by a horse during a boar hunt.

plays.title: Dagobert and Saint Florentius
plays.description: Hermit Florentius bounds wild beasts with wooden rods and cures King Dagobert's blind and mute daughter.

plays.title: Dagobert's Soul in the Boat
plays.description: Saints intercept devils on the sea, rescuing King Dagobert's soul from being carried to hell.

plays.title: Dagobert and His Dogs
plays.description: French popular proverbs remember King Dagobert's deep companionship and parting words to his dogs.

plays.title: The Two Identical Sons
plays.description: King Pepin tricks his second queen with her identical stepson, resolving a maternity dilemma.

plays.title: Hildegard
plays.description: Empress Hildegard traps the treacherous stepbrother Taland in a bridal chamber, defending her honor.

plays.title: The Cockfight
plays.description: Queen Hildegard settles the royal succession dispute between her sons using a cockfight.

plays.title: Charlemagne's Return from Hungary
plays.description: Charlemagne's signet ring dropped in a cup rescues Queen Hildegard from a forced remarriage.

plays.title: The Stag of Magdeburg
plays.description: Charlemagne releases a stag with a golden collar, which is only recaptured centuries later by Barbarossa.

plays.title: The Lombard Minstrel
plays.description: A minstrel sings of a secret pass and guides Charlemagne's army over the mountains to defeat the Lombards.

plays.title: The Iron Charlemagne
plays.description: King Desiderius watches in terror from a tower as the iron-clad army of Charlemagne approaches.

plays.title: Charlemagne Besieges Pavia
plays.description: A princess betrays Pavia for Charlemagne's love, only to be trampled to death by the entering army.

plays.title: Adelgis
plays.description: The disguised Prince Adelgis splits marrow bones at Charlemagne's table and escapes in a boat.

plays.title: Of King Charles and the Frisians
plays.description: Charlemagne wins Frisia from King Radbod in a standing contest by picking up a dropped glove.

plays.title: Radbod Refuses Baptism
plays.description: Duke Radbod pulls his foot out of the baptismal font to avoid spending eternity apart from his pagan ancestors.

plays.title: The Devil's Golden House
